title: Caramelized Moroccan Tangerine Stir-fry
description: A tangy and creamy crowd-pleaser featuring Thai green curry paste, caramelize the tangerines for a unique twist that's raw-friendly.

ingredients.name: Ground beef or tofu for protein
ingredients.notes: Use a high-quality protein source that aligns with dietary preferences

ingredients.name: Canned tangerines (optional)
ingredients.notes: Choose unsweetened and low-sodium versions, if available

ingredients.name: Water or vegetable broth
ingredients.notes: For best results use non-dairy alternatives for a vegan option

ingredients.name: Vegetable oil
ingredients.notes: Ensure it's safe to consume raw

instructions: Prepare the tangerines by washing them and halving. Use a spoon to scrape out the seeds and membranes.
Place the tangerine halves in a shallow baking dish. Drizzle with oil, cover with foil, and bake for about 15 minutes or until they are caramelized.
Meanwhile, mix all curry paste ingredients (except those mentioned earlier) in a bowl. Heat oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the protein and sauté until browned. Then add bell pepper and chopped green onions to the pan for 2-3 minutes or until softened.
Pour in the tangerine juice, stir curry paste mixture into the pan with all ingredients. Simmer for about 5 minutes until thickened.
Garnish with fresh herbs if desired.
